title: Notes from the Watering Can
synopsis: A watering can observes as young Chloe receives seven mysterious gifts throughout a week in her secret rooftop garden, each one bringing delightful animal visitors and teaching her to put down her tablet and embrace the natural world around her.

pages.text:
Well, I never expected to have stories to tell, but here I am—a copper watering can with quite the tale. You see, I spend my days watching over this secret garden, perched high above the rumbling city where car horns honk and sirens wail below. Up here, though, it's different. The tomatoes grow fat and red, the mint spreads like green gossip, and young Chloe comes to visit with her tablet clutched tight in her small hands.

She always sits in the same spot by the lettuce, tapping that glowing screen while I wonder if she even notices the butterflies dancing around her head. Today feels different, though. There's a small brown package sitting right next to me, tied with string and no return address.

illustration.description: A gouache painting showing a rooftop garden with thick brushstrokes in warm savanna colors. Chloe, with short wavy strawberry-blonde hair, light skin, and blue-gray eyes, sits among vegetables and herbs, looking at a tablet. A copper watering can is prominently featured next to a mysterious brown package. The city skyline is visible below with expressive painterly textures and golden light.
illustration.alt: Chloe in a rooftop garden with a mysterious package

pages.text:
Chloe arrives just as a pigeon lands near the tomatoes. She doesn't look up from her tablet until she nearly trips over me—clumsy as always, that one. "Oh!" she says, noticing the package. "What's this?"

She opens it carefully, and out tumbles a small cloth pouch filled with sunflower seeds. There's a note too, written in careful handwriting: "For Monday's friends. —A garden watcher."

Chloe looks around, puzzled, then shrugs and sprinkles the seeds near the fence. Within minutes, three sparrows arrive, chirping cheerfully as they feast. For the first time all afternoon, Chloe sets her tablet down and watches them eat.

illustration.description: A gouache painting with thick brushstrokes showing Chloe with short wavy strawberry-blonde hair, light skin, and blue-gray eyes, sprinkling seeds while three sparrows gather around her feet. The rooftop garden is painted in warm savanna colors with golden grass tones. Her tablet lies forgotten beside her in the scene painted with bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Chloe feeding sparrows while her tablet sits unused

pages.text:
Tuesday brings another package, this one containing a tiny ceramic dish and a note: "For the thirsty visitors." I watch Chloe fill it with water from my spout—finally, someone appreciates my purpose! She places it by the marigolds.

A striped cat appears within the hour, lapping delicately while Chloe giggles at its pink tongue. "You're so polite," she tells the cat, who purrs in response. Her tablet screen goes dark from neglect.

Wednesday's gift is stranger still: a small mirror on a stick with instructions to "Place where the morning sun hits brightest." Chloe, being wonderfully clumsy, drops it twice before finding the perfect spot near the bean plants.

illustration.description: A gouache painting showing Chloe with short wavy strawberry-blonde hair, light skin, and blue-gray eyes, kneeling next to a striped orange cat drinking from a ceramic water dish. The scene is painted with thick brushstrokes in warm savanna colors. Vegetables and flowers surround them in the rooftop garden, painted with expressive textured painterly style in golden grass tones.
illustration.alt: Chloe watching a striped cat drink water in the garden

pages.text:
The mirror catches the sunrise and sends dancing light patterns across the garden walls. A curious robin lands right on the mirror's edge, tilting its head this way and that at its reflection. Chloe laughs so hard she snorts, which makes her laugh even more.

"You silly bird," she says, "that's just you!"

I notice her tablet hasn't been touched since she arrived. Instead, she's been talking to the robin like an old friend, telling it about her week while it chirps back as if it understands every word. Perhaps it does—birds are clever creatures, after all.

illustration.description: A gouache painting with bold confident strokes showing a red robin perched on a small mirror, with Chloe (short wavy strawberry-blonde hair, light skin, blue-gray eyes) laughing nearby. Light patterns dance on the garden walls painted in warm savanna colors. The scene has thick brushstrokes and golden sunset tones with expressive painterly textures.
illustration.alt: A robin looking at itself in a mirror while Chloe laughs

pages.text:
Thursday's package contains dried corn kernels and the note: "For the bold ones." Chloe scatters them near the pepper plants, and I'm not surprised when a pair of squirrels appears, chittering excitedly as they stuff their cheeks full.

One squirrel is particularly brave, sitting up on its hind legs right next to Chloe's sneaker. She reaches out slowly and almost—almost!—touches its bushy tail before it scampers away.

Friday brings a small bundle of fresh lettuce leaves "for the hoppers," and Saturday delivers the most peculiar gift yet: a tiny wooden box with air holes and a note reading, "Handle with care—friend inside."

illustration.description: A gouache painting showing Chloe with short wavy strawberry-blonde hair, light skin, and blue-gray eyes, sitting cross-legged while two squirrels eat corn nearby. One squirrel is very close to her shoe. The rooftop garden is painted with thick brushstrokes in warm savanna colors and golden grass tones, with expressive animals rendered in bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Chloe sitting near two squirrels eating corn in the garden

pages.text:
Chloe opens the box carefully, and out crawls a small green caterpillar. "Oh!" she gasps, then whispers, "Hello there, little guy."

She places it gently on a cabbage leaf and watches as it immediately begins munching. The rabbit that comes for Friday's lettuce is brown with white spots, nibbling contentedly while Chloe lies on her stomach, chin in her hands, studying both creatures.

"You're all so different," she murmurs to her growing audience of garden visitors. "But you all know exactly what you need."

Her tablet, I notice, has been forgotten in her backpack all day.

illustration.description: A gouache painting with textured painterly style showing Chloe (short wavy strawberry-blonde hair, light skin, blue-gray eyes) lying on her stomach watching a small green caterpillar on a cabbage leaf and a spotted brown rabbit eating lettuce. The scene uses warm savanna colors with thick brushstrokes and golden grass tones, painted with expressive and bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Chloe lying down watching a caterpillar and rabbit in the garden

pages.text:
Sunday arrives with the biggest package yet. Inside, Chloe finds seven small wooden signs, each painted with a different animal, and a final note: "For labeling the neighborhoods in your garden. Every creature needs a home. —Your friend, Mrs. Chen from apartment 4B."

Chloe gasps. "Mrs. Chen! But I've never even met her!"

She looks up at the apartment windows surrounding our rooftop garden, and there in one window, an elderly woman waves. Chloe waves back enthusiastically, nearly knocking me over in her excitement—some things never change.

"She's been watching us all week," Chloe realizes, looking around at all the animal visitors who've made themselves at home.

illustration.description: A gouache painting showing Chloe with short wavy strawberry-blonde hair, light skin, and blue-gray eyes, waving up at an apartment window where an elderly woman waves back. The rooftop garden is full of various animals - birds, squirrels, a cat, and a rabbit. Small wooden signs are scattered around. The scene is painted with thick brushstrokes in warm savanna colors and golden sunset tones with bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Chloe waving at Mrs. Chen while surrounded by garden animals

pages.text:
Chloe spends the afternoon placing the signs throughout the garden: "Sparrow Station" by the fence, "Cat Café" near the water dish, "Squirrel Snack Bar" by the peppers. As she works, all her new friends gather around—the sparrows perching on her shoulders, the cat rubbing against her legs, the squirrels chattering from the tomato stakes.

When she's finished, she sits back and surveys her transformed garden. Her tablet pokes out of her backpack, but she doesn't reach for it. Instead, she picks me up and gives my handle a pat.

"Thank you for keeping everything watered," she says. "I think we make a pretty good team."

Indeed we do, young Chloe. Indeed we do.

illustration.description: A gouache painting with expressive animals and thick brushstrokes showing the complete rooftop garden scene. Chloe with short wavy strawberry-blonde hair, light skin, and blue-gray eyes sits surrounded by all her animal friends - sparrows on her shoulders, cat at her feet, squirrels nearby, rabbit munching lettuce. Small wooden signs label different areas. The watering can sits prominently nearby. The scene uses warm savanna colors with golden grass tones and bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Chloe surrounded by all her animal friends in the completed garden with signs

pages.text:
From my spot by the mint, I watch Chloe visit every day now. She still brings her tablet, but it stays in her backpack more often than not. Instead, she talks to the sparrows, plays peek-a-boo with the cat, and has learned to sit perfectly still when the rabbit comes to eat.

Mrs. Chen visits too, bringing fresh water for me and wisdom for Chloe. "Plants and animals teach us to pay attention," she says one afternoon. "They live in the moment—no screens required."

Chloe nods seriously, then grins as the brave squirrel runs up her arm to sit on her shoulder. "They're better than any video," she agrees.

And from where I sit, watching over this secret garden above the noisy city, I couldn't agree more.

illustration.description: A final gouache painting showing the thriving rooftop garden community. Chloe with short wavy strawberry-blonde hair, light skin, and blue-gray eyes sits with Mrs. Chen among all the animals. The watering can sits contentedly nearby. The city bustles below while their peaceful garden sanctuary thrives above, painted with thick brushstrokes in warm savanna colors and golden light with textured painterly style and bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: The complete garden community with Chloe, Mrs. Chen, and all the animals together

animalFacts: Sparrows can remember where they found food and return to the same spots
Squirrels can leap 10 times their body length
Cats have a special scent organ that helps them analyze smells
Rabbits can see almost 360 degrees around them

coverPrompt.description: A gouache painting showing Chloe, a girl with short wavy strawberry-blonde hair, light skin, and blue-gray eyes, sitting in a lush rooftop garden surrounded by plants and flowers. A copper watering can sits prominently in the foreground. The city skyline stretches below with thick brushstrokes in warm savanna colors. Golden afternoon light bathes the scene with bold confident strokes.
coverPrompt.alt: A girl in a rooftop garden with a watering can in the foreground

questions.question: Who was sending the mysterious gifts to Chloe?
questions.explanation: Mrs. Chen reveals herself at the end as the 'garden watcher' who had been sending gifts all week.

questions.question: What was the first gift that Chloe received?
questions.explanation: Monday's gift was a pouch of sunflower seeds for the sparrows.

questions.question: Who tells the story in this book?
questions.explanation: The watering can narrates the entire story, watching over the garden and observing Chloe's adventures.

questions.question: What happened to Chloe's tablet as the week went on?
choices: She used it less and less
questions.explanation: As Chloe became more interested in the animals and garden, she gradually stopped using her tablet as much.

questions.question: What did the Sunday gift contain?
questions.explanation: Sunday's gift contained seven wooden signs painted with different animals to label the garden neighborhoods.
